title: Chapter 66 – Fame
content:
Hyeon Won-chang stammered.

Agitation swept across Wei Ji Myo-hwa's face beside him.

Shock broke through even the cultivation she had forged at Mount Zhongnan, sacred ground of the Dao Gate.

Cheong Myeong spoke slowly.

In the murim, fifteen years was enough for an entire generation of young masters to turn over. It meant she had not displayed her divine might in a long time.

Some young people—rising talents who had built deep inner power through spirit medicine—were said to look down on older generations.

But martial arts was a discipline that deepened with the passage of time.

No disciple of a renowned orthodox lineage feared the peerless martial arts of centuries past.

Desolate Sect's lord was a different existence entirely.

Wei Ji Myo-hwa murmured, her fingers stroking her sword hilt. The shock ran deep. Even her worry for Jeong Yeon-shin had been briefly scattered.

Cheong Myeong glanced at her, then gestured to Hyeon Won-chang.

Hyeon Won-chang exclaimed as he left the room.

A human deity was descending—one whose arrival even the expression

fell short of describing.

TL Note- *it basically means strong military might.

The prefecture governor who oversaw all of Huizhou stood three ranks above the magistrate.

His status rivaled that of the Namgung family head. Even so, he would have to pay his respects before Desolate Sect's lord.

For she was a queen recognized by the emperor.

Hyeon Won-chang relayed Cheong Myeong's words to the magistrate, then watched with satisfaction as the man's face drained of color.

This magistrate was closely tied to the Namgung family, local nobles of South Zhili.

His previously lukewarm response to requests for government troops vanished without a trace.

When Hyeon Won-chang emerged from the work room, something caught his attention.

A middle-aged swordsman in blue martial robes stood in the main hall. His glare at Hyeon Won-chang carried an unusual edge.

Though he did not seem to have consciously raised his energy, the layered waves permeating the air were oppressively heavy.

'Azure Sky Boundless Sword!'

Hyeon Won-chang immediately recognized the Namgung family's signature technique. Even the clothing matched those damned people's.
The swordsman with a neatly trimmed jet-black beard spoke.

Hyeon Won-chang held his silence for a moment.

He knew the name well. This was the vice leader of the renowned Azure Sky Sword Heaven Group.

The man was said to be one of the finest swordsmen in South Zhili—a master that even most blue-ranked experts could not be certain of defeating.

'My neck could fly off if I am not careful.'

The Namgung family was ranked above even the Hwangbo family.

Judging by the magistrate's behavior, their collusion with local government ran deep.

They had the power to bury an incident even if they committed murder inside a government office.

Hyeon Won-chang carefully opened his mouth.

The anger accumulated over time flowed out in a calm, measured tone.

A gentle aura spread from his body.

Namgung Wi's reaction was delayed by a single breath.

As Hyeon Won-chang's words continued, the internal energy waves rising from Namgung Wi's body intensified.

Hyeon Won-chang paid them no heed.
He concluded with a refreshed expression. Namgung Wi shook his head.

WOONG.

His sword began to emit a majestic cry.

Indeed, even the sword's resonance was extraordinary for a renowned swordsman.

Hyeon Won-chang was about to fill his throat with true qi and call for Cheong Myeong and Wei Ji Myo-hwa.

Thud.

There was no cutting sound.

With only a gentle breeze, Namgung Wi's head separated from his body. It fell without ceremony.

It was as natural as a leaf drifting to the ground.

Opposite the rolling head, the body of the renowned swordsman collapsed.

So futile was the end that for an instant it did not seem real.

Since when?

A ceaseless breeze, gentle as heat haze, circled through the hall. The moment was deeply strange.

Though the streams of wind were colorless, it felt as if faint green rippled outward in every direction.

Time in the government office seemed frozen. A quiet transcendence was blooming in the silence-drenched hall.
A melodious voice rang out from the rooftop.

TL Note- they are talking about burying Lightning Flash solely based on their assumptions that he was captured and killed.

Long robes the color of tender grass fluttered overhead.

Hyeon Won-chang had already tilted his head skyward. His eyes widened as if they might tear.

He knelt with perfect bearing, but excitement trembled through his entire body.

* * *

A playful whisper tickled his ear. Thick inner power laced the breath. He was accustomed to it by now—the Seventh Apostle's exhale.

Jeong Yeon-shin opened his eyes at once. His senses did not sharpen immediately.

Though he could have raised his entire body's energy sense in an instant simply by willing it, his true qi activation was momentarily sluggish.

He knew at once. He had been subdued.

'My paralysis points were sealed again.'

The Seventh Apostle's face blocked half his vision. Her expression brimmed with deep affection. Desire flickered there too.

Jeong Yeon-shin gazed quietly at her red outer eye before speaking.

TL Note- The child she is referring to is Young Jeong Yeon-shin, nothing else. People might make needless assumptions.

She smiled brightly and straightened her posture.

Only then did Jeong Yeon-shin detect the stimulating scent of ink and paint.

Was she skilled in painting and calligraphy? His entire body was rendered on the large sheet of paper in the Seventh Apostle's hand.

It was not merely a portrait. It reproduced his internal anatomy.

The full network of meridians—including the eight extraordinary vessels and blood pathways—and even the three dantians. One section was particularly striking.

It was the upper dantian of his head, connected to blood pathways that opened wide from his crown. The detail was astonishing.

An anatomical diagram.

She had observed his body by channeling true qi through it.

The Seventh Apostle was a master of internal energy techniques. Scanning his body would have been trivial for her.

The corners of her mouth rose.

The conversation did not mesh. It was a relationship forged by the murim's law of survival of the fittest.

The smiling Seventh Apostle and the calm Jeong Yeon-shin had been misaligned from the start.

He closed his eyes again and focused his internal energy on the lower dantian.

The Moon Spirit Harmony Technique transmitted by Desolate Sect's lord was said to be a peerless martial art, rare in all the world.

He had spent considerable time training to forge a new internal energy cultivation technique with extremely rapid energy accumulation.

His insight had deepened merely from studying and examining the verses.

Indeed—Jeong Yeon-shin was already familiar with supreme breathing methods.

'So this is how my conversation with the Sect Lord about internal energy cultivation comes back to me.'

The verses of the Demon Roaring Blood Technique—examined immediately after receiving the secret manual from the Blood Flame Sect Leader—unfolded in his mind.

He had largely classified the effects of the energy accumulation technique.

He could distinguish which verses affected the brain first. He removed them immediately.

The Seventh Apostle asked cautiously. She seemed to have taken her earlier outburst and repeated apologies to heart.

Jeong Yeon-shin ignored her and familiarized himself with the Demon Roaring Blood Technique's energy circulation.

He did as the Seventh Apostle wished. He preserved the nature of the blood technique true qi she had planted in his body.

'It is done.'

After dismantling the verses, the rest was instantaneous. A new true qi pathway was engraved in his mind.

He could now purge the Seventh Apostle's true qi that held his lifeline sealed within his meridians.

Additionally, he learned something.

'The blood techniques' regenerative ability originates from true qi absorption.'

The Sect Leader's warning returned to him. He had said not to touch the fundamentals. The Blood Flame Sect's martial arts were two branches from one root.

Even knowing the technique, it could not be openly used outside.

Jeong Yeon-shin was already considering escape.

He parted his lips slowly.
The Seventh Apostle's face flooded with pure, white joy. She nodded repeatedly.

As she spoke, she brought over a neatly folded package and placed it in his hands. It was red.

From its thickness and shape, it was a robe—like the clothes she often wore.
His Desolate Sect martial clothes had already been confiscated. Jeong Yeon-shin looked down at the package before speaking.

The Seventh Apostle asked with a slight tilt of her head, but Jeong Yeon-shin offered no answer.

He had formed a plan after studying the formation yesterday.

He needed to move through the grounds and plant energy in various still objects.

How did she interpret his silence? The Seventh Apostle cycled through expressions on her own.

Perhaps because of his words about working on the Demon Roaring Blood Technique, anger and possessiveness she could not freely express were both present.

There seemed to be a trace of regret as well.

'It is not for my sake. It is because of her own desires.'

It was an emotion Jeong Yeon-shin had no need to agonize over. Whatever pity she felt must stem from her identity as a Blood Flame Sect member.

Shaking his head inwardly, he pushed open the door.

The voice that had been grating on his ears since midday suddenly grew louder.

The Seventh Apostle spoke in a peculiar tone.

Red hair filled every direction. There seemed to be easily hundreds of Blood Flame Sect members.

The man spouting passionate words from a platform in the village center suddenly turned his head. His gaze met Jeong Yeon-shin's.

In that instant, the corners of his mouth curled upward.

A tremendous number of gazes followed Gao Zong-xue's gesture. Even the Seventh Apostle turned to look, smiling softly.

True qi was loaded into Gao Zong-xue's continuing shout.

The miracle he spoke of seemed to mean modifying the Blood Summoning Technique verses, but the demand was unreasonable.

It had been only one day since regaining consciousness and spending a night in the main hall.

'Even the Blood Flame Sect Leader did not speak of immediate results.'

This was the Third Apostle's son. In that moment, Jeong Yeon-shin realized there was political strife even in evil sects.

Gao Zong-xue intended to exploit the existing reputation to thoroughly embarrass him. Was even a group of madmen a place where ordinary human ambitions lived?

From what the Seventh Apostle had said in the Sect Leader's hall, the Third Apostle and his son were vying for the position of small sect leader.

Their hunger for power cut through even the blood techniques' madness.

Jeong Yeon-shin shook out the package he was holding. The bright red cloth unfolded and fluttered like a flag.

He began putting on the robe in silence, layer by layer.

With a few measured movements, the Pure Blood Robe settled over his body, its hem rippling blood-red.

The crowd fell silent. Gao Zong-xue was no different.

Jeong Yeon-shin's appearance was that strange.

WOONG.

Was it because he had begun circulating the Demon Roaring Blood Technique with the madness excised?

It was an aura never before felt from the Blood Flame Sect.

Though clearly the energy waves of blood techniques, a transparent current created an atmosphere uniquely eerie—unmistakably of the Blood Flame Sect, yet somehow purified.

Some sect members whispered. Words flew from every direction—Sect Leader, Grand Teacher, Apostle, Pure Blood…

The energy waves radiating from his body stirred the wind. Black hair swayed against the shoulders of the red robe.

In that moment, his bearing was indistinguishable from the Blood Flame Sect's orthodox martial lineage.
He asked.
